name: Interference Cancellation & Mitigation
description: Sub-sector template for interference cancellation, suppression, and mitigation patents. Covers successive interference cancellation (SIC), inter-cell interference coordination (ICIC), self-interference cancellation for full-duplex, and co-existence interference management.
contextDescription: Interference is the fundamental limiter of wireless capacity. Every cellular device and base station implements interference management. Key techniques: SIC in the receiver, ICIC/eICIC at network level, self-interference cancellation for full-duplex. 5G NR dense deployments (small cells) make interference management critical. Key targets: Qualcomm (receiver algorithms), Intel, Samsung, all infrastructure vendors (Ericsson, Nokia). Full-duplex communication is a major research area where self-interference cancellation is the key enabler.
scoringGuidance: Interference cancellation is performed in every cellular receiver — enormous infringement surface.
SIC (successive interference cancellation) patents are widely implemented in LTE-A receivers.
Inter-cell interference coordination (eICIC, FeICIC) patents affect every HetNet deployment.
Self-interference cancellation for full-duplex is high-value emerging technology.
Co-existence interference (WiFi/LTE, radar/5G) patents are relevant for shared spectrum.

questions.question: What interference cancellation technique does this patent use? Score 8-10 for self-interference cancellation (full-duplex) or advanced SIC with machine learning, 5-7 for standard SIC or MMSE-IRC, 3-4 for basic interference suppression, 1-2 for no specific technique.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Full-duplex self-interference cancellation is the highest-value emerging technique.

questions.question: Does this patent address network-level interference coordination (ICIC, eICIC, CoMP)? Score 8-10 for novel coordinated scheduling across cells, 5-7 for enhanced ICIC (ABS patterns, power control), 3-4 for basic frequency reuse, 1-2 for receiver-only techniques.
questions.reasoningPrompt: ICIC is mandatory in every HetNet deployment — small cells co-existing with macro cells.

questions.question: In which domain does the interference cancellation operate? Score 8-10 for combined analog+digital cancellation (100+ dB), 6-7 for analog domain (RF/baseband), 4-5 for digital domain only, 2-3 for spatial domain (beamforming), 1 for no specific domain.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Combined analog+digital cancellation achieves the deepest cancellation for full-duplex.

questions.question: Does this patent address co-existence interference between different systems (WiFi/LTE, radar/5G, CBRS)? Score 8-10 for dynamic spectrum sharing interference management, 5-7 for inter-system co-existence techniques, 3-4 for adjacent channel interference, 1-2 for single-system only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Shared spectrum (CBRS, 6 GHz WiFi/5G) makes co-existence increasingly important.

questions.question: How detectable is this interference cancellation innovation? Score 8-10 for observable in receiver performance metrics (SINR improvement) or network signaling (ICIC), 5-7 for detectable via chip teardown (cancellation circuits), 3-4 for firmware/algorithm level, 1-2 for theoretical only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Receiver IC architecture and ICIC signaling are both detectable — identify the evidence path.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with 3GPP interference management procedures? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of standardized IC/ICIC behavior, 5-7 for enabling standard compliance, 3-4 for standard-adjacent, 1-2 for proprietary.
questions.reasoningPrompt: ICIC signaling is standardized in 3GPP — SIC receiver types are defined in UE categories.
